title: Long Voyages in a Fragmented World: The Evolution of Tankers
section_path: Long Voyages in a Fragmented World: The Evolution of Tankers
section_path_text: Long Voyages in a Fragmented World: The Evolution of Tankers
summary:
Main
Geopolitical shocks and trade fragmentation and refinery-production relocations have lengthened tanker voyages, and increased freight volatility. By Ioannis Papadimitriou
Over the past four years, tanker markets have been reshaped by geopolitical shocks, with freight volatility increasingly driven by inefficiencies rather than pure cycles. The Russian invasion of Ukraine triggered a major reconfiguration of crude and product flows, extending voyage distances and tightening effective fleet supply.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
Geopolitical shocks and trade fragmentation and refinery-production relocations have lengthened tanker voyages, and increased freight volatility. By Ioannis Papadimitriou
Over the past four years, tanker markets have been reshaped by geopolitical shocks, with freight volatility increasingly driven by inefficiencies rather than pure cycles.

children.title: Longer routes and tighter fleet driven by geopolitical fragmentation
section_path: Longer routes and tighter fleet driven by geopolitical fragmentation
children.section_path_text: Longer routes and tighter fleet driven by geopolitical fragmentation
children.summary: The first major shift followed the Russian invasion of Ukraine, following EU and US bans on Russian oil that triggered a fundamental reshuffling of crude and product flows during the start of 2023.Russian crude moved east toward China and India, while Europe increased reliance on the Atlantic Basin. In products, middle distillates saw the largest disruption: Russian diesel was redirected to more distant markets such as West Africa and Brazil, while Europe turned toward the Middle East, India, and the US.

children.title: Production growth and refinery shifts reshaping Americas tanker flows
section_path: Production growth and refinery shifts reshaping Americas tanker flows
children.section_path_text: Production growth and refinery shifts reshaping Americas tanker flows
children.summary: Shifting refinery configurations, terminal expansions, and production growth have further reshaped flows, particularly in the Americas. For Aframaxes, Mexico's push for self-sufficiency reduced short-haul exports to the US Gulf, which in 2026 is partly offset by increased Venezuelan heavy barrels following the easing of OFAC restrictions, both events supported longer distances.

children.title: Strait of Hormuz risk reshaping tanker flows, prolonged closure harmful for rates
section_path: Strait of Hormuz risk reshaping tanker flows, prolonged closure harmful for rates
children.section_path_text: Strait of Hormuz risk reshaping tanker flows, prolonged closure harmful for rates
children.summary: More recently, tensions between the United States and Iran and the resulting disruption in the Strait of Hormuz have once again rewritten tanker flows. Supply constraints in Asia have triggered a sharp shift of Atlantic Basin barrels into the Pacific, tightening vessel availability and sustaining rate volatility.

children.title: Outlook: structurally stronger tanker demand despite geopolitical uncertainty
section_path: Outlook: structurally stronger tanker demand despite geopolitical uncertainty
children.section_path_text: Outlook: structurally stronger tanker demand despite geopolitical uncertainty
children.summary: Looking ahead, uncertainty remains high, but several trends are evident. A normalisation of flows through the Strait of Hormuz would restore Middle East exports and ease routing inefficiencies.
